issues.title: chatModel cannot be null when adding SimpleLoggerAdvisor
issues.body:
**Bug description**
When creating a `ChatClient` without explicitly specifying a `ChatModel` (relying on auto-configuration), the call to the LLM fails if a default advisor is set (e.g., `SimpleLoggerAdvisor`). The following exception is thrown:

```
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: chatModel cannot be null
```

**Environment**
- Spring AI version: 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
- Java version: GraalVM 21.0.4 - VM 23.1.4
- Impacted models: VertexAIGemini, Mistral

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Create a ChatClient without specifying a `ChatModel`, using an injected `ChatClient.Builder` and calling `chatClient.build()`.
2. Add a default advisor (e.g., SimpleLoggerAdvisor).
3. Call the LLM, specifying the model via the prompt.

> Note: Everything works as expected if no default advisor is set.

**Expected behavior**
The call to the LLM should succeed, and the advisor should be invoked correctly during execution.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
```kotlin
 package com.malt.platform.spring.test.controller

 import org.springframework.ai.chat.client.ChatClient
 import org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.SimpleLoggerAdvisor
 import org.springframework.ai.chat.prompt.ChatOptions
 import org.springframework.ai.chat.prompt.Prompt
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController

 @RestController
 @RequestMapping("/springai-test")
 class SpringAiTestController(
    chatClientBuilder: ChatClient.Builder,
 ) {
    private val chatClient: ChatClient = chatClientBuilder.defaultAdvisors(SimpleLoggerAdvisor()).build()

    @PostMapping("/springai")
    fun springAi(
        @RequestBody prompt: String,
    ): String? =
        chatClient
            .prompt(
                Prompt
                    .Builder()
                    .chatOptions(
                        ChatOptions.builder().model("gemini-2.0-flash-001").build(),
                    ).build(),
            ).user(prompt)
            .call()
            .content()
 }
```

comments.body:
@mautini thanks for reporting this issue. Could you provide some more details about the error, such as the stack trace?

I tried reproducing the error using the same code you shared, but I couldn't reproduce it (using Java).

Hi @ThomasVitale, thanks for taking a look!

Here’s a more complete stack trace:

```
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: chatModel cannot be null
	at org.springframework.util.Assert.notNull(Assert.java:172) ~[spring-core-6.1.14.jar:6.1.14]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.api.AdvisedRequest.<init>(AdvisedRequest.java:93) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.api.AdvisedRequest$Builder.build(AdvisedRequest.java:430) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.api.AdvisedRequest.from(AdvisedRequest.java:191) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.lambda$nextCall$1(DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.java:105) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
	at io.micrometer.observation.Observation.observe(Observation.java:564) ~[micrometer-observation-1.15.0-RC1.jar:1.15.0-RC1]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.nextCall(DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.java:100) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.lambda$doGetObservableChatClientResponse$1(DefaultChatClient.java:508) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
```

Following your comment, I did some digging. I noticed that with a minimal demo app, everything works as expected. However, the issue arises when I add the Spring Boot Actuator starter dependency:

```kotlin
implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-actuator")
```

Could you try adding it on your end and see if you’re able to reproduce the issue?
@mautini thanks a lot for the additional information. I'll give it a further look and share an update later today.
I managed to reproduce it. I'm working on a fix. Thanks so much for reporting this!

> I managed to reproduce it. I'm working on a fix. Thanks so much for reporting this!

Hi, this discussion method this issue too, maybe can give you some information too

@[https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2810](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2810)
